title: Angel Ecosystem split
wikitext:
[]: Angel Ecosystem [split]             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jun 15, 2010 2:32 pm [Split from here . Some of the context-establishing posts needed to be left behind, unfortunately, so I edited some stuff in as quotes.] AuraTwilight wrote: Adam was never meant to have a "set" number of children or an "end to her lifecycle" like you seem to think. If not for Lilith's presence fucking things up, the planet would be FULL of Angels, not just 15.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Jun 16, 2010 9:32 am Aura Twilight wrote: And if some of them die? Oops, no way to replenish ecosystem lolz.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 16, 2010 3:29 pm just want to point out something i've heard from you about a thousand times: just because that's the way it works with us, doesn't mean it's the same for them. the EVA universe has a whole different set of rules. monk ed has a point: we don't know if they can actually die of natural causes, all we know is that they die if you destroy their core but if there's no external force around to do that, maybe they do live forever.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jun 16, 2010 3:38 pm AuraTwilight wrote: That's all well and good, but what about fights? Or accidents? What if a fucking meteor whams an Angel right in the core, or something? Angels CAN die, and if you're giving a species absolutely no way to take care of itself, it's hardly anything at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 16, 2010 4:24 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Technically no. Israfel was two souls in one Angel, complete with two cores. They're more like conjoined twins.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Jun 17, 2010 11:55 am as much as i like the idea of angels fighting each other (surely i can't be the only one who thinks this is awesome ), even the idea that they can die accidentally doesn't imply some form of reproduction is needed. mammoth nuclear bomb goes off. giant meteor hits the planet. earth axis is completely turned over. deadly virus epidemic. zombie apocalypse. chickens go crazy and start eating people (and everything else). whatever floats your boat-- but let's say life on earth dies, and guess what, our reproductive systems were worth shit in the end. you (the creator if he/she exists, or the FAR in the EVA universe) can't account for every single accident that might occur. living beings (eh, lilim in this case) have different forms of reproduction because we die naturally . regardless of how it happens, the probability of death for each creature currently living on earth (again, i'm talking about lilim) is 100%. as such there is the need to pass on our genetic information before we inevitably die (however it happens) so the species doesn't die out. if death isn't inevitable for angels, there's no way of telling how much of a natural imperative reproduction is. besides, when it comes to accidents, as legendary pointed out, they have their ATFs, that's how they take care of themselves. the only way for them to die would be to kill each other, and although it could certainly happen, we have no way of knowing how likely it will be. even from an evolutionary PoV, reproduction isn't strictly necessary for them because they can take whatever form/shape/property they want, so it's not like reproduction implies the "betterment" of the species either. point is, while it may make sense for us, in our experience, that angels should have some way to reproduce... there's no definitive evidence that they do, or even that they have to. we just can't know. have to say, though, that as always, i loved the "mommy?!" imagery. lol, never fails to crack me up...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:18 pm as much as i like the idea of angels fighting each other (surely i can't be the only one who thinks this is awesome), even the idea that they can die accidentally doesn't imply some form of reproduction is needed. mammoth nuclear bomb goes off. giant meteor hits the planet. earth axis is completely turned over. deadly virus epidemic. zombie apocalypse. chickens go crazy and start eating people (and everything else). whatever floats your boat-- but let's say life on earth dies, and guess what, our reproductive systems were worth shit in the end. you (the creator if he/she exists, or the FAR in the EVA universe) can't account for every single accident that might occur.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:55 pm Why in the world would the FAR just decide "Oh, they don't need it. Fuck'em"?
